title: Forward Freight Agreement (FFA) and Freight Risk Management, a powerful mix
section_path: Forward Freight Agreement (FFA) and Freight Risk Management, a powerful mix
section_path_text: Forward Freight Agreement (FFA) and Freight Risk Management, a powerful mix
summary:
Main
The daily fluctuations of shipping freight rates are of great importance to shipowners and charterers involved in the transportation of bulk commodities. The question is how the interested parties can manage the intense volatility and the freight risk to minimize the negative impact of fluctuations on the profitability of a vessel's employment contract. The freight market is no different in its functionality from any other market like commodities or the stock exchange.

children.title: Main
children.summary: The daily fluctuations of shipping freight rates are of great importance to shipowners and charterers involved in the transportation of bulk commodities. The question is how the interested parties can manage the intense volatility and the freight risk to minimize the negative impact of fluctuations on the profitability of a vessel's employment contract.

children.title: The Freight Derivatives Market
children.summary: The freight derivatives market, in contrast to the physical, also bears a lot of practical problems to be resolved. It is important to highlight that there are large sums of money involved as well as high risks, and the market must be liquid enough to allow contracts to be placed reasonably quickly.

children.title: The mechanism of a Forward Freight Agreement (FFA)
section_path: The mechanism of a Forward Freight Agreement (FFA)
children.section_path_text: The mechanism of a Forward Freight Agreement (FFA)
children.summary: Forward Freight Agreements are actually futures contracts that allow shipping market participants to trade on an expected future level of freight rates. They are derived from the physical, spot freight market, widely used in the dry bulk and tanker freight segments.

children.title: What is a Forward Freight Agreement?
section_path: What is a Forward Freight Agreement?
children.section_path_text: What is a Forward Freight Agreement?
children.summary: FFA is a contract between two counterparties to settle a freight rate for a specified quantity of cargo for one of the major dry bulk or tanker shipping routes at a certain date in the future. The underlying route can be any of the routes that constitute the indices produced by the Baltic Exchange.

children.title: Where are FFA contracts trading?
children.summary: The FFAs are trading over the counter market (OTC) on the terms and conditions of the Forward Freight Agreement Broker Association (FFABA) standard contracts. These are contracts negotiated between a broker and two counterparties (shipowner - charterer), in a manner similar to a conventional physical market negotiation.

children.title: Futures and Options
children.summary: The freight derivatives are traded either as freight futures or options at different expiry dates, while the clearing is taking place at major clearinghouses, under specific requirements. For the dry FFA contracts, the clearing exchanges are the European Energy Exchange (EEX) and the Singapore Exchange (SGX).

children.title: What's the difference between Futures and Options?
section_path: What's the difference between Futures and Options?
children.section_path_text: What's the difference between Futures and Options?
children.summary: Freight option contracts are financial agreements with the same main characteristics as futures contracts, i.e. different expiry dates on the forward curve starting from the first month and up to six calendar years, cleared on a regulated financial exchange.

children.title: FFA | The Dry Bulk Freight Market, Signal indicators versus FFAs
section_path: FFA | The Dry Bulk Freight Market, Signal indicators versus FFAs
children.section_path_text: FFA | The Dry Bulk Freight Market, Signal indicators versus FFAs
children.summary: As mentioned earlier not all freight routes are liquid in the FFA market. Dry bulk FFA contracts for Capesize, Panamax, and Supramax have significant liquidity.

children.title: Vessel Supply & Demand - Freight Rates
children.summary: The vessel supply evolution determines the supply curve of seaborne transportation, thus the daily information on vessels' availability at key loading areas is crucial for the evolution of freight rates in line with the demand for shipping services. The demand is connected to the industrial production levels linked to raw materials and the economic growth in developed and developing economies.

children.title: The Signal Ocean FFA Report | Overview of FFA prices across all routes and contracts
section_path: The Signal Ocean FFA Report | Overview of FFA prices across all routes and contracts
children.section_path_text: The Signal Ocean FFA Report | Overview of FFA prices across all routes and contracts
children.summary: Options and Futures rates are shown through graphs in all main dry bulk ship sizes, Capesize, Panamax, Supramax, and Handysize, for all key trading routes, per month, quarter and year, for the expired and non-expired contracts. The forward prices are quoted for the spot ($/t)-Image 1 and time charter rates ($/d)- Image 2.
